data.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer↔consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 coverage finding; no uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

8937 is the KEYSTONE/story B and OWNS the largest share of parent criteria — including the very criterion (the documented anti-change-detector guardrail) that earlier roster audits mis-filed as a gap before its named owner was recognized.

OWNED — FULLY covered by THIS child:
- Parent AC "A single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema module exists as the canonical shape" → 8937 AC1 (`_snapshot_schema.py` defines TypedDict + JSON Schema as the SSOT, covering summary/description(ADF)/status/priority/assignee/labels/comment(nested)/issuelinks/parent). FULLY.
- Parent AC "A test validates the fetcher's snapshot output against that schema" → 8937 AC2 (validates `fetcher.fetch_snapshot` output vs schema; would FAIL on pre-fix conditions — issuelinks absent; comment under the wrong key). FULLY.
- Parent AC "A test validates each differ's snapshot reads against that schema" → covered by 8937 AC1 (schema covers every field the differs read) + AC3 (the round-trip drives inbound_differ + outbound_differ reads through the production path against the schema-validated snapshot, asserting comment/issuelinks/parent + scalars read correctly). FULLY.
- Parent AC "One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher → snapshot → differs → applier through the production path, using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" → 8937 AC3 (round-trip through the PRODUCTION path; fixtures-half supplied by fe57, which 8937 declares depends_on). FULLY (test → 8937; fixtures → fe57).
- Parent AC "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" → 8937 AC5 + description ("Owns the epic's guardrail artifact (resolves the roster-level gap): commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail … a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it"). The parent AC NAMES story B = THIS child (8937) as owner verbatim; THIS child delivers it. FULLY. (NOTE: the recurring "AC6 PARTIALLY covered — no sibling produces a governing guardrail" finding raised on sibling tickets earlier was a FALSE POSITIVE against a paraphrased "governs new tests" wording and has been WITHDRAWN across 2359/33d0/59ef/822a; it is explicitly resolved HERE because 8937 is the named owner and AC5 commits the artifact referenced by new tests.)
- Parent SC "Producer (fetcher + enrichment) and consumers (differs/appliers) cannot drift on snapshot shape without a test failing" → 8937 (schema SSOT + producer-validation AC2 + joint round-trip AC3, complemented by fe57's honest fixtures and 59ef's verified-fake fidelity). FULLY.
- Parent SC "New tests assert behavior/contract (semantic round-trip, structure/type); the suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added" → 8937 AC4 (all assertions semantic — keys/types/round-trip — never whole-blob golden equality or interaction/mock-call-count) + the committed guardrail (AC5) that governs the posture. FULLY.

DELEGATION — parent criteria 8937 does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; 8937's declared OUT items (capturing fixtures → story A/fe57; collapsing appliers → story D/33d0; telemetry → story E/2359) are consistent with the roster:
- AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path (no parallel applier remains)" + AC "The retired outbound applier path is deleted" + SC "Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path" → 33d0 (D).
- AC "Outbound sub-op telemetry (links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied) is surfaced in the structured batch outcome" + AC "A `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly, after the warn-first rollout" + SC "A silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green" → 2359 (E).
- AC "Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip is asserted hermetically through the production path" + AC "Every probe assertion that encodes a current bug as expected is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes" + SC "Bidirectional round-trips … are asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a (F).
- Real-Jira-payload fixtures + FakeAcliClient (the fixture half of the round-trip AC) → fe57 (A); verified-fake fidelity (real-vs-fake dual-run) → 59ef (C).

4-bucket summary for this pairing:
- FULLY (OWNED by THIS child): SSOT schema (AC1); fetcher-snapshot-validated-vs-schema (AC2); each-differ's-reads-validated-vs-schema (AC1+AC3); round-trip contract test through the production path (AC3, test-half); documented anti-change-detector guardrail (AC5, named owner); SC "producer/consumers cannot drift"; SC "semantic assertions / lean / zero change-detector".
- COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING: applier collapse + retired-path deletion + production-path exercise (33d0); sub-op telemetry + fail-loud canary (2359); bidirectional hermetic round-trip + probe de-encoding (822a); real-payload fixtures/fake (fe57); verified-fake fidelity (59ef).
- STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE: parent AC "All six child stories are closed; the full reconciler suite is green."
- PARTIALLY / UNCOVERED: none.

No G3 coverage finding for this pairing. The union of children covers every parent acceptance/success criterion; THIS child (8937) fully delivers its owned share (SSOT schema + fetcher/differ schema validation + the production-path round-trip contract test + the documented anti-change-detector guardrail) and is the named owner that resolves the previously mis-filed roster-level guardrail concern.
